sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports experiencing significant pain in his left back for approximately three days, with the pain radiating lower. He states he has not passed the stone yet and is in considerable pain. He also reports nausea and some chills. He has had recurrent kidney stones, estimating this is his third episode. The patient reports that previous stones typically pass in 3-4 days. He has tried Tylenol and ibuprofen, but finds them ineffective. He has not been consistent with taking his blood pressure regularly, having taken it at least three times a week, but has recorded readings ranging from one-thirties to one-forty with a bottom number between seventy and eighty. He enjoys french fries and would like assistance with dietary changes.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam reveals tenderness to palpation in the abdomen, particularly in the right flank. A grade 2/6 systolic ejection murmur is auscultated. Lung sounds are clear bilaterally. Blood pressure was elevated at 180/120 prior to the visit. The patient's A1C was 7.3% and he is currently taking 500mg of metformin daily. His glucose levels before meals have been in the low 100s.
medical_concepts: tenderness to palpation in the abdomen

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient has a history of hypertension and diabetes. He is experiencing a kidney stone, with inflammation around the affected kidney. The current pain is likely related to the kidney stone and inflammation.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Rescribe oxycodone 5 mg every 6-8 hours as needed for pain. Continue Tylenol as needed for breakthrough pain. Instruct the patient to strain urine to monitor for stone passage. Refer to urology for further evaluation and management of recurrent kidney stones. Continue norvasc 2.5 mg daily. Refer to a registered dietitian for dietary counseling to assist with salt intake and overall dietary changes. Schedule follow-up in urology in 2-3 days after stone passage, or sooner if symptoms persist.

medical_concepts: tenderness to palpation in the abdomen
